focus: Driver yips, mental blocks, fear-based dysfunction, confidence collapse, considering quitting golf

PAIN.quote: I have the driver yips & it is resulting in my seriously considering giving up the game. It started about 3 years ago & has crept to the point that I stand on the tee & know there is absolutely no way I am going to hit the ball where I can play the hole from
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Help! I've got the driver yips
PAIN.context: Driver yips driving golfer to quit

PAIN.quote: forget the fairway, I'm not even hitting it into the rough where I can punch out & finish the hole. Mostly low, nasty, short pullhooks with the occasional huge block to the right
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Help! I've got the driver yips
PAIN.context: Can't even play from the rough

PAIN.quote: I'm 58, been playing since I was 11 & mostly to a low single-digit handicap. Currently carrying a 6.7 that I can't play anywhere close to because I had 3-4 rounds late last summer where I actually kept the ball in play. Three of my last eight rounds have been in the 90s
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Help! I've got the driver yips
PAIN.context: Lifetime golfer shooting 90s due to driver

PAIN.quote: The back used to be my favorite side because if you keep the ball in play there are more birdie opportunities, but now I dread making the turn
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Help! I've got the driver yips
PAIN.context: Driver yips turned favorite holes into dread

PAIN.quote: Driver yips take enjoyment out of the game for many people. My son quit for years because of them. He was a D1/D2 level golfer, too
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Help! I've got the driver yips
PAIN.context: D1 golfer quit due to driver yips

PAIN.quote: I have been hitting low pulls and wipey fades over the past month that are well below normal ball flight usually in the 200-220 window

I had several days of striping the driver at the range and great launch and distance and then I get out to the course and can't hit it to save my life. I seem to have multiple issues going on with it... I fix one item and the next rears its ugly head
PAIN.context: Range success, course failure - whack-a-mole problems

PAIN.quote: I have worked on driver for what seems like years, finally got fitted and hit the club well then but haven't been able to sort it out since. I am honestly about to just start over with the driver again and go back to starting fresh
PAIN.context: Years of work, fitting didn't solve it

HOPE.quote: I decided on a more reasonable course of action to deal with this issue -> new driver, obviously. First round out with Ping G425 Max yesterday was a great trial. The forgiveness and ball speed consistency is unbelievable
I would recommend testing one of the PING max models. It takes a really bad swing to put you into trouble. The difference in confidence overnight is staggering

One thing I realized yesterday afternoon on the practice tee is the longer I stand over the ball with my driver, the more likely I am to hit it off the world. Staying in motion, shifting weight from back foot to front & back again, one waggle & start the swing seems to help
HOPE.source: GolfWRX - Help! I've got the driver yips
HOPE.context: Stay in motion routine helps yips

HOPE.quote: tee the ball up lower....maybe half the height you'd normally tee it for a driver. That's why Laura Davies hits a driver off the dirt...she had wild control problems with a driver off the tee. Maybe it's a crutch, but it actually works
HOPE.source: GolfWRX - Help! I've got the driver yips

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: A yip is an involuntary spasm of one or more muscles during the swing or stroke. It is actually an attempt by the subconscious mind to prevent a bad shot from happening. The fear of the bad shot is the root cause
ROOT_CAUSE.source: GolfWRX - Help! I've got the driver yips

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: The word 'yip' doesn't have much to do with technique. It's a mental issue, probably caused by thinking of the wrong things over the ball and during the swing
ROOT_CAUSE.source: GolfWRX - Help! I've got the driver yips

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: It has gone from being a straightforward mechanical/technique problem to a mental block
ROOT_CAUSE.source: GolfWRX - Help! I've got the driver yips
ROOT_CAUSE.context: Mechanical problem evolved into mental block

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: the 'feeling' that you're yippy leads me to believe your tempo with the driver is a wreck.... and 100% that you are BADLY short stroking the shoulder turn in the back swing
ROOT_CAUSE.source: GolfWRX - Help! I've got the driver yips

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: It feels to me like I am not releasing the club on course, shifting toward target on downswing and trapping it with my standard out to in path after having a period of huge slices earlier in the year
ROOT_CAUSE.context: Not releasing on course vs practice

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: Went in for a lesson, adjusted grip weaker and ball position forward to how I started the season when hitting well and was hitting normal carry distances around 250-260. Had range session later in the week, experimented with shorter shaft in driver

I think it started as I began to lose a little distance off the tee in an effort to regain it. I started trying to be more aggressive with the lower body & have gotten to the point I feel like I'm driving my left foot into the ground, stalling my left hip
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: GolfWRX - Help! I've got the driver yips

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: When I was playing all the time and low single digits, I played an old school 2 iron off the tee. I knew long par 5's were 3 shots but had the confidence to play it that way

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: Used it indoors one night to get used to the feel, 90% of shots were within a 10 yard range of carry distance. The MOI is so high it is hard to zero in on where exactly you are striking it as balls don't always leave a well defined mark

Hit 2 bad drives: 1 wipey heel cut that curved back into the fairway and ended up 220 and a low pull that ran through the trees and into the rough on an adjacent hole. Both allowed for clear shots into the green
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.context: Max forgiveness - bad shots still playable

VILLAIN.quote: Yips, pretty much by definition, are mental/emotional in their root cause. The current bias in golf instruction to view every bad shot as caused ONLY by poor mechanics reflects a really huge lack of understanding of how the mind, brain and body work together
VILLAIN.source: GolfWRX - Help! I've got the driver yips

VILLAIN.quote: I specialize in helping golfers with the yips, and how to address the syndrome at it's root causes. In the past three months, I have coached four golfers with severe yips (ten on a one to ten scale) that had been a problem for 8-12 year time span, and cured every one of them
VILLAIN.source: GolfWRX - Help! I've got the driver yips
VILLAIN.context: Yips can be cured - but takes specialized help

VILLAIN.quote: I've gotten my hopes up too many times
VILLAIN.source: GolfWRX - Help! I've got the driver yips

sources_covered: GolfWRX - Help! I've got the driver yips
high_value_themes_identified: D1 golfer quit for years due to yips
Mechanical problem evolves into mental block
